layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market but no clean swing trade setup exists right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with a Fear & Greed score of 26, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.2%, meaning altcoins are getting crushed relative to BTC. The swing trade candidates available are either in clear downtrends with bearish chart structures, or have already pumped hard this week and look extended. While a couple of coins like TRUMP and ZRO show oversold RSI readings, their charts are firmly bearish with price below all moving averages — catching a falling knife without a confirmed reversal signal is not a high-probability trade.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is fearful but hasn't shown a clear bounce signal yet. Better setups will emerge once selling pressure exhausts and we see volume-confirmed reversals.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all 10 candidates systematically. TRUMP (RSI 29) and ZRO (RSI 31) are the most oversold, but both have chart_bias_score of -0.70, price below all MAs, and are in confirmed downtrends — classic falling knives. GRASS dropped 24.8% in 7 days which could trigger a mean-reversion bounce, but the bearish engulfing pattern and -0.90 chart bias score warn against entry. VELVET surged 36% in 24h but has a poor risk/reward ratio of 0.26x and a slightly bearish chart bias. High BTC dominance (56.2%) is hostile to alt positions. My signal credibility data shows volume_breakout signals have a 100% win rate — none of these candidates show volume breakout confirmation. Without that, entering any of these is speculation, not a high-probability setup.
risk_factors: High BTC dominance (56.2%) continues to drain capital from altcoins, making alt swing trades higher risk
Geopolitical tensions (U.S.-Iran strikes) could trigger sudden risk-off moves across all crypto
Stablecoin market cap shrinking by $10B since May suggests capital is leaving the crypto ecosystem
watch_list: TRUMP and ZRO for RSI reversal confirmation — if RSI bounces off oversold with a bullish candle pattern and volume spike, these become actionable
Bitcoin's behavior at $64K — a decisive move above $66K or below $62K will set the tone for altcoin direction
Fear & Greed index — currently 26 and improving; a move below 20 could mark capitulation and a high-conviction buy zone
market_context: Bitcoin is holding near $64K in a fearful market (F&G: 26) with high dominance at 56.2%. Altcoins are broadly weak, and the stablecoin market shrinkage suggests cautious capital flows — not the environment for aggressive alt swing trades.
context_banner: Market fear is elevated but not yet at capitulation levels — patience is the highest-conviction trade right now.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 ('Fear') and improving from weekly avg of 24 — market is fearful but not yet at capitulation levels where blind buying becomes profitable.
TRUMP RSI at 29 (oversold) and ZRO at 31 (near-oversold) are the strongest technical signals, but both lack volume confirmation and have bearish chart structures.
Bitcoin nearing Fidelity's power law support line tracked since 2015 suggests BTC may find a floor, but the U.S.-Iran strikes add geopolitical risk that could override technicals.
Bitcoin's $64K rebound is outrunning ETF demand despite $197M inflow — suggesting the rally lacks institutional conviction and could fade.
ETH derivatives reset noted by Blockworks suggests positioning has been cleaned up, which could set up a healthier move once direction is confirmed.
